019d31a8-9d88-7c03-bda1-b2377939aecf.completed:
### Strengths
- Workspace integration is clean: new `@openfons/shared` package has clear entrypoint/barrel and TS build/type outputs ([package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/package.json#L2), [index.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/index.ts#L1)).
- Root wiring is minimal and coherent for this stage (`workspace:*` dependency + root `tsconfig.json`) ([package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/package.json#L16), [tsconfig.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tsconfig.json#L1)).
- Baseline contract tests exist for all three helpers and current CI scripts pass (`pnpm check` succeeded) ([shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L1)).

### Issues
#### Critical (Must Fix)
- `createId` does not guarantee an 8-char suffix, but the contract assumes fixed length. Current implementation uses `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)`, which can produce shorter strings (example: `Math.random=()=>0.5` yields `opp_i`) ([id.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/id.ts#L2), [shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L16)).

#### Important (Should Fix)
- The contract test does not actually validate package export behavior (`dist`/`exports`). Because Vitest uses `vite-tsconfig-paths`, `@openfons/shared` resolves to source (`packages/shared/src/index.ts`) rather than package `exports` target, so packaging regressions can slip through ([vitest.config.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/vitest.config.ts#L5), [tsconfig.base.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tsconfig.base.json#L13), [packages/shared/package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/package.json#L8)).

#### Minor (Nice to Have)
- `slugify` test coverage is only happy-path and misses key edge cases (empty/whitespace-only input, repeated separators, non-ASCII text), reducing confidence in downstream usage ([text.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/text.ts#L1), [shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L9)).

### Recommendations
- Make `createId` length deterministic (`padEnd`/`slice` strategy or switch to UUID-based generation) and add deterministic tests by stubbing randomness.
- Add one packaging-level test that imports `@openfons/shared` via Node resolution against built output (`dist`) to verify `exports` actually works.
- Expand helper tests with edge-case matrices, especially for `slugify`.

### Assessment
Ready to merge? With fixes  
Reasoning: Core scaffolding is good, but there is one correctness bug in ID generation and one meaningful packaging-test gap that should be addressed before considering this stable.

Because Vitest uses `vite-tsconfig-paths`, `@openfons/shared` resolves to source (`packages/shared/src/index.ts`) rather than package `exports` target, so packaging regressions can slip through ([vitest.config.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/vitest.config.ts#L5), [tsconfig.base.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tsconfig.base.json#L13), [packages/shared/package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/package.json#L8)).

#### Minor (Nice to Have)
- `slugify` test coverage is only happy-path and misses key edge cases (empty/whitespace-only input, repeated separators, non-ASCII text), reducing confidence in downstream usage ([text.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/text.ts#L1), [shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L9)).

### Recommendations
- Make `createId` length deterministic (`padEnd`/`slice` strategy or switch to UUID-based generation) and add deterministic tests by stubbing randomness.
- Add one packaging-level test that imports `@openfons/shared` via Node resolution against built output (`dist`) to verify `exports` actually works.
- Expand helper tests with edge-case matrices, especially for `slugify`.

### Assessment
Ready to merge? With fixes

input.message:
Please fix the verified Task 2 review issues in the same worktree.

Verified by independent checks:
1. Critical bug: after `pnpm --filter @openfons/shared build`, running built code with `Math.random = () => 0.5` produces `opp_i`, so `createId` does NOT guarantee an 8-character suffix.
2. Packaging gap: `node --input-type=module` can import `@openfons/shared` after build, but our tests do not currently verify that package-exports path.

Fix scope (only Task 2 areas):
- packages/shared/**
- tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts
- add one packaging-level contract test under tests/contract/**
- root lockfile only if it changes naturally (unlikely)

Required fixes:
- Make `createId` deterministically return an 8-character suffix even for short base36 output.
- Strengthen tests so this bug is caught deterministically (stub randomness and assert exact output or exact 8-char behavior).
- Add one packaging-level contract test that verifies `@openfons/shared` resolves through Node/package exports against built output, not just tsconfig path alias.

Required verification before reporting back:
- `pnpm test -- tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- tests/contract/*.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @openfons/shared lint`
- `pnpm check`
- `git status --short`

Do not amend. Create a new commit on top.
Report back with status, exact verification results, changed files, and new commit SHA.

previous_status.completed:
### Strengths
- Workspace integration is clean: new `@openfons/shared` package has clear entrypoint/barrel and TS build/type outputs ([package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/package.json#L2), [index.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/index.ts#L1)).
- Root wiring is minimal and coherent for this stage (`workspace:*` dependency + root `tsconfig.json`) ([package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/package.json#L16), [tsconfig.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tsconfig.json#L1)).
- Baseline contract tests exist for all three helpers and current CI scripts pass (`pnpm check` succeeded) ([shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L1)).

### Issues
#### Critical (Must Fix)
- `createId` does not guarantee an 8-char suffix, but the contract assumes fixed length. Current implementation uses `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)`, which can produce shorter strings (example: `Math.random=()=>0.5` yields `opp_i`) ([id.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/id.ts#L2), [shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L16)).

#### Important (Should Fix)
- The contract test does not actually validate package export behavior (`dist`/`exports`). Because Vitest uses `vite-tsconfig-paths`, `@openfons/shared` resolves to source (`packages/shared/src/index.ts`) rather than package `exports` target, so packaging regressions can slip through ([vitest.config.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/vitest.config.ts#L5), [tsconfig.base.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tsconfig.base.json#L13), [packages/shared/package.json](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/package.json#L8)).

#### Minor (Nice to Have)
- `slugify` test coverage is only happy-path and misses key edge cases (empty/whitespace-only input, repeated separators, non-ASCII text), reducing confidence in downstream usage ([text.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/packages/shared/src/text.ts#L1), [shared-utils.test.ts](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/openfons/repo-skeleton-v1/tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts#L9)).

### Recommendations
- Make `createId` length deterministic (`padEnd`/`slice` strategy or switch to UUID-based generation) and add deterministic tests by stubbing randomness.
- Add one packaging-level test that imports `@openfons/shared` via Node resolution against built output (`dist`) to verify `exports` actually works.
- Expand helper tests with edge-case matrices, especially for `slugify`.

### Assessment
Ready to merge? With fixes  
Reasoning: Core scaffolding is good, but there is one correctness bug in ID generation and one meaningful packaging-test gap that should be addressed before considering this stable.

input.message:
Please fix the Task 3 spec-compliance issue in the same worktree.

Spec reviewer found one issue:
- `packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json` exceeded scope by adding `compilerOptions.paths` for `@openfons/shared`.

Required fix:
- Remove that unauthorized `paths` override so `packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json` matches the planned shape exactly.

Allowed verification work:
- You may run any needed commands to confirm the package still works after removing the override.
- Do not broaden scope beyond removing that override and any lockfile changes if a command unexpectedly touches it.
- Do not amend. Create a new commit on top.

Required report back:
- Status
- What you changed
- Exact verification results (`pnpm test -- tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts`, `pnpm --filter @openfons/domain-models lint`, and if needed any supporting build command you ran)
- New commit SHA
- Any concerns

- Do not amend. Create a new commit on top.

- Status
- What you changed
- Exact verification results (`pnpm test -- tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts`, `pnpm --filter @openfons/domain-models lint`, and if needed any supporting build command you ran)
- New commit SHA
- Any concerns"}

input.message:
Please apply a minimal follow-up fix in the same worktree.

I independently verified a cleaner fix:
- If `packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json` adds `compilerOptions.paths: {}` (empty object), `pnpm exec tsc -p <temp-config> --noEmit` succeeds.
- This clears the inherited root source aliases and lets package-local NodeNext resolution use the actual workspace package path instead of pulling `packages/shared/src/*` under `rootDir`.

Authorized minimal deviation for Task 3:
- You may add `compilerOptions.paths: {}` to `packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json`.
This replaces the removed dist-path hack and should be treated as the narrowest possible fix for package-local lint correctness.

Required verification:
- `pnpm test -- tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @openfons/domain-models lint`
- `pnpm check`
- `git status --short`

Do not amend. Create a new commit on top.
Report back with status, exact verification results, changed files, and new commit SHA.

Spec compliance review for Task 3 final state.

Repo: C:\Users\ai\.config\superpowers\worktrees\openfons\repo-skeleton-v1
Base: ab1881b
Head: fefc9c8

## Original Task 3 Requirements
Files:
- Create: packages/domain-models/package.json
- Create: packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json
- Create: packages/domain-models/src/index.ts
- Test: tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts

Required package.json content:
- name @openfons/domain-models
- dependency on @openfons/shared workspace:*
- build/lint/typecheck scripts

Required tsconfig.json planned shape:
- extends ../../tsconfig.base.json
- compilerOptions module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, declaration true, rootDir src, outDir dist
- include src/**/*

Required src/index.ts content:
- TopicStatus, ArtifactType, Topic, Evidence, EvidenceSet, Artifact
- createEvidenceSet using createId('es') and nowIso
- addEvidence immutable append

Required test content:
- verifies empty evidence set for topic
- verifies addEvidence is immutable

Authorized narrow expansions during execution:
- root package.json may add `@openfons/domain-models": "workspace:*"` in devDependencies for package resolution
- pnpm-lock.yaml may update naturally
- cleanup of packages/domain-models/src/.gitkeep is allowed
- AFTER integration verification showed inherited root `paths` aliases broke package-local `tsc` with TS6059, one additional minimal deviation was explicitly authorized: `packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json` may add `compilerOptions.paths: {}` to clear inherited root source aliases and restore package-local NodeNext/workspace resolution without adding dist-specific hacks

## Your Job
Review actual final implementation at fefc9c8 and determine whether Task 3 is spec compliant under the updated authorized boundary.

Output exactly:
PASS or FAIL
If FAIL: specific issues
If PASS: brief explanation
